mission.title: Install and Configure Openkruise on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenKruise is a CNCF incubating project that automates the management of large-scale applications on Kubernetes. It provides advanced workload management capabilities, making it easier to handle stateless, stateful, and job applications efficiently.

steps.title: Add the OpenKruise Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenKruise Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kruise https://charts.kruise.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenKruise using Helm
steps.description:
Install OpenKruise in a new namespace called 'kruise-system' with the following command:
```bash
helm install kruise kruise/kruise --namespace kruise-system --create-namespace --version v1.8.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the OpenKruise pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kruise-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for OpenKruise
steps.description:
Edit the deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment configuration:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kruise-controller-manager -n kruise-system
```
Add the following under the 'spec.template.spec.containers' section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for OpenKruise
steps.description:
Apply the necessary RBAC permissions for OpenKruise with the following command:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openkruise/kruise/master/deploy/rbac.yaml
```

resolution.summary: After completing these steps, you should have OpenKruise installed and running in your Kubernetes cluster. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods in the 'kruise-system' namespace.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kruise https://charts.kruise.io
helm repo update
helm install kruise kruise/kruise --namespace kruise-system --create-namespace --version v1.8.3

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OpenKruise release from the Kubernetes cluster using Helm with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall kruise --namespace kruise-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with OpenKruise and any persistent volumes if applicable:
```bash
kubectl delete crd kruise*.kruise.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'kruise-system' namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kruise-system
kubectl get namespaces | grep kruise-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the OpenKruise installation. You can use the following command to export the current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kruise-system -o yaml > kruise-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade OpenKruise to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kruise kruise/kruise --namespace kruise-system --version v1.9.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the OpenKruise pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kruise-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If OpenKruise pods are stuck in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the failing pod:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kruise-system
```
Investigate the logs for errors and adjust the configuration as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during the Helm upgrade, check the currently installed version:
```bash
helm list --namespace kruise-system
```
Ensure that the version you are trying to upgrade to is compatible with the existing installation.

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permissions issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you experience RBAC permission issues, verify the applied RBAC configuration:
```bash
kubectl get clusterrolebindings | grep kruise
kubectl get roles -n kruise-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing pod evictions
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are being evicted due to resource limits, check the resource usage:
```bash
kubectl top pods -n kruise-system
```
Adjust the resource limits in the deployment configuration as necessary.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
